research.title: CVE-2026-27886: Unauthenticated Boolean-Oracle Exfiltration of Administrator Secrets in Strapi
research.summary: A sanitization bypass in Strapi 4.0.0 through 5.36.1 lets unauthenticated attackers extract an admin's password reset token character by character and take over the account. With over 20,000 internet-facing hosts exposed, Bishop Fox breaks down how the boolean oracle technique works and the full exploitation chain.

research.title: Detecting CVE-2026-0265 at Scale: PAN-OS CAS Authentication Bypass
research.summary: CVE-2026-0265 lets unauthenticated attackers forge a JWT and log in as any trusted user on CAS-enabled PAN-OS deployments. Bishop Fox breaks down how the authentication logic fails and how a single anonymous request can yield a definitive verdict.

vulns.title: Drupal Core SQL Injection Vulnerability
vulns.summary: Drupal Core PostgreSQL SQL Injection — anonymous SQL injection through the JSON:API module on PostgreSQL-backed sites, allowing unauthenticated data exfiltration.
vulns.technicalSummary: CVE-2026-9082 is a highly critical SQL injection vulnerability affecting Drupal core sites configured to use PostgreSQL as the backend database. The vulnerability exists in Drupal's database abstraction API, where specially crafted requests can bypass parameterization and reach raw query execution.
vulns.breakdown: CVE-2026-9082 is a highly critical SQL injection vulnerability affecting Drupal core sites configured to use PostgreSQL as the backend database. The vulnerability exists in Drupal's database abstraction API, where specially crafted requests can bypass parameterization and reach raw query execution.
vulns.whatBroke: Drupal's database abstraction layer failed to fully sanitize attacker-controlled input passed through the JSON:API module on PostgreSQL backends, allowing unauthenticated SQL injection.
vulns.reality: Realistic when the affected product is exposed and unpatched. The updater only includes this because public tooling signal was found at 7h30th3r0n3/CVE-2026-9082-Drupal-PoC.
vulns.exploitSyntax:
# authorized lab / owned targets only
git clone https://github.com/7h30th3r0n3/CVE-2026-9082-Drupal-PoC
cd CVE-2026-9082-Drupal-PoC
python3 <script>.py --target https://<authorized-target>
# confirm exact flags in the linked GitHub README before validation
chains: unauthenticated SQL injection via JSON:API module
credential and session data exfiltration from database
admin account takeover via extracted password hashes
webshell upload through authenticated file manager
lateral movement from compromised Drupal host
vulns.weaponized: Likely worth tracking because public tooling exists and recent source activity was observed.

vulns.title: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller Authentication Bypass Vulnerability
vulns.summary: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller and Manager authentication bypass rated CVSS 10.0 — unauthenticated attackers can gain full control of the SD-WAN management plane.
vulns.technicalSummary: An authentication bypass in the Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager allows unauthenticated remote attackers to access the management interface without valid credentials. With a CVSS score of 10.0, exploitation gives direct control over SD-WAN policy, routing, and connected branch infrastructure.
vulns.breakdown: An authentication bypass in the Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager allows unauthenticated remote attackers to access the management interface without valid credentials. With a CVSS score of 10.0, exploitation gives direct control over SD-WAN policy, routing, and connected branch infrastructure.
vulns.whatBroke: The Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller and Manager failed to enforce authentication on a critical management interface, allowing unauthenticated access to the full SD-WAN control plane.
vulns.reality: Realistic when the affected product is exposed and unpatched. The updater only includes this because public tooling signal was found at portbuster1337/CVE-2026-20182.
vulns.exploitSyntax:
# authorized lab / owned targets only
git clone https://github.com/portbuster1337/CVE-2026-20182
cd CVE-2026-20182
python3 <script>.py --target https://<authorized-target>
# confirm exact flags in the linked GitHub README before validation
chains: unauthenticated access to SD-WAN management plane
SD-WAN policy and routing table manipulation
traffic interception or redirection across branch sites
credential access from management interface
lateral movement to connected branch infrastructure
vulns.weaponized: Likely worth tracking because public tooling exists and recent source activity was observed.

vulns.title: Local Root Privilege Escalation and Credential Disclosure in the Linux Kernel ptrace Path
vulns.summary: A logic flaw in the Linux kernel's __ptrace_may_access() function permits an unprivileged local user to escalate to root and disclose credential material from other processes.
vulns.technicalSummary: The Qualys Threat Research Unit (TRU) has discovered and published the full advisory for CVE-2026-46333, a logic flaw in the Linux kernel's __ptrace_may_access() function that permits an unprivileged local user to read credential material and escalate privileges to root.
vulns.breakdown: The Qualys Threat Research Unit (TRU) has discovered and published the full advisory for CVE-2026-46333, a logic flaw in the Linux kernel's __ptrace_may_access() function that permits an unprivileged local user to read credential material and escalate privileges to root.
vulns.whatBroke: A logic flaw in Linux kernel's __ptrace_may_access() dumpability check allows an unprivileged local attacker to cross process trust boundaries and access credential material belonging to privileged processes.
vulns.reality: Realistic when the affected product is exposed and unpatched. The updater only includes this because public tooling signal was found at portbuster1337/lpe-toolkit.
vulns.exploitSyntax:
# authorized lab / owned targets only
git clone https://github.com/portbuster1337/lpe-toolkit
cd lpe-toolkit
make
./<compiled-poc> <authorized-target>
# confirm exact flags in the linked GitHub README before validation
chains: local foothold on Linux host
ptrace-based credential disclosure from privileged processes
local privilege escalation to root
lateral movement if secrets or management access are recovered
vulns.weaponized: Likely worth tracking because public tooling exists and recent source activity was observed.

vulns.title: Langflow Unauthenticated Code Injection Vulnerability
vulns.summary: Langflow contains a code injection vulnerability that allows unauthenticated attackers to build and execute public flows, reaching remote code execution on the server.
vulns.technicalSummary: Langflow contains a code injection vulnerability that allows unauthenticated attackers to build and execute public flows, reaching remote code execution on the server.
vulns.breakdown: Langflow contains a code injection vulnerability that allows unauthenticated attackers to build and execute public flows, reaching remote code execution on the server.
vulns.whatBroke: Langflow's flow execution engine accepted attacker-controlled code through the public flow API without requiring authentication, leading to server-side code execution.

vulns.title: Next.js Server-Side Request Forgery via WebSocket Upgrade
vulns.summary: Next.js WebSocket upgrade handling SSRF — crafted requests cause the server to forward connections to localhost:80, enabling pre-auth credential theft from internal services and cloud metadata endpoints.
vulns.technicalSummary: CVE-2026-44578 is a server-side request forgery vulnerability in self-hosted Next.js applications using the built-in Node.js server. Crafted WebSocket upgrade requests cause the server to forward the connection to localhost:80, giving unauthenticated access to internal APIs and cloud metadata services.
vulns.breakdown: CVE-2026-44578 is a server-side request forgery vulnerability in self-hosted Next.js applications using the built-in Node.js server. Crafted WebSocket upgrade requests cause the server to forward the connection to localhost:80, giving unauthenticated access to internal APIs and cloud metadata services.
vulns.whatBroke: Next.js WebSocket upgrade request handling failed to validate the target host, allowing attacker-controlled upgrade requests to reach localhost-bound services including cloud metadata endpoints.
vulns.reality: Realistic when the affected product is exposed and unpatched. The updater only includes this because public tooling signal was found at dinosn/CVE-2026-44578.
vulns.exploitSyntax:
# authorized lab / owned targets only
git clone https://github.com/dinosn/CVE-2026-44578
cd CVE-2026-44578
python3 <script>.py --target https://<authorized-target>
# confirm exact flags in the linked GitHub README before validation
chains: unauthenticated SSRF via crafted WebSocket upgrade request
access to cloud metadata endpoint (AWS IMDSv1, GCP, Azure)
credential theft from metadata service or internal API
lateral movement using stolen cloud credentials or service tokens
vulns.weaponized: Likely worth tracking because public tooling exists and recent source activity was observed.

vulns.title: Docker Container Escape via mlx-metal importlib
vulns.summary: Docker container escape via mlx-metal importlib path abuse — allows a process inside a container to break out to the host and access co-located workloads.
vulns.technicalSummary: Docker container escape via mlx-metal importlib path abuse — allows a process inside a container to break out to the host and access co-located workloads.
vulns.breakdown: Docker container escape via mlx-metal importlib path abuse — allows a process inside a container to break out to the host and access co-located workloads.
vulns.whatBroke: A path abuse vulnerability in mlx-metal's Python importlib handling allows attacker-controlled code running inside a Docker container to escape the container boundary and gain access to the host.
vulns.reality: Realistic when the affected product is exposed and unpatched. The updater only includes this because public tooling signal was found at davidrxchester/CVE-2026-5843.

container boundary escape to host via importlib abuse
access to host filesystem and co-located container data
credential theft from mounted secrets or environment variables
lateral movement to cluster infrastructure or other pods
vulns.weaponized: Likely worth tracking because public tooling exists and recent source activity was observed.

vulns.title: FreeBSD 14.x setcred Local Privilege Escalation
vulns.summary: FreeBSD 14.x setcred(2) stack buffer overflow — the kernel copies user-supplied supplementary groups into a fixed-size stack buffer before checking caller privileges, enabling local privilege escalation to root.
vulns.technicalSummary: The setcred(2) system call on FreeBSD 14.x copies a user-supplied list of supplementary groups into a fixed-size kernel stack buffer before checking whether the caller is privileged. This allows an unprivileged local attacker to overflow the buffer and gain root.
vulns.breakdown: The setcred(2) system call on FreeBSD 14.x copies a user-supplied list of supplementary groups into a fixed-size kernel stack buffer before checking whether the caller is privileged. This allows an unprivileged local attacker to overflow the buffer and gain root.
vulns.whatBroke: FreeBSD's setcred(2) syscall performed a bounds-unchecked copy of attacker-controlled supplementary group data into a kernel stack buffer before privilege validation, creating a stack overflow LPE path.
vulns.reality: Realistic when the affected product is exposed and unpatched. The updater only includes this because public tooling signal was found at venglin/setcred.
vulns.exploitSyntax:
# authorized lab / owned targets only
git clone https://github.com/venglin/setcred
cd setcred
make
./<compiled-poc> <authorized-target>
# confirm exact flags in the linked GitHub README before validation
chains: local foothold on FreeBSD 14.x host
setcred stack overflow to kernel privilege escalation
root access and credential harvesting
lateral movement if secrets or management access are recovered
vulns.weaponized: Likely worth tracking because public tooling exists and recent source activity was observed.
